content:
# Mark Chapter 9 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), folios 36v–37v (PDF pages 72–74) | **Source Image:** `source-texts/greek-NT/MRK_009_page_001.jpg`, `source-texts/greek-NT/MRK_009_page_002.jpg`, `source-texts/greek-NT/MRK_009_page_003.jpg`
**Secondary Witness:** Codex Vaticanus (B, 03) — consulted via [DigiVatLib](https://digi.vatlib.it/view/MSS_Vat.gr.1209)

---

¹ And he-was-saying to-them, "Truly I-say to-you that there-are some of-those standing here who will not taste death until they-see the kingdom of God having-come in power."

² And after six days, Jesus takes-along Peter and James and John and leads them up into a-high mountain privately alone. And he-was-transfigured before them,

³ and his garments became glistening, exceedingly white, such-as a-fuller on the earth is not able thus to-whiten.

⁴ And there-appeared to-them Elijah with Moses, and they-were conversing with Jesus.

⁵ And answering, Peter says to Jesus, "Rabbi, it-is good for-us to-be here; and let-us-make three tents — one for-you and one for-Moses and one for-Elijah."

⁶ For he-did not know what to-answer, for they-became terrified.

⁷ And there-came a-cloud overshadowing them, and there-came a-voice out-of the cloud, "This is my Son, the beloved. Hear him."

⁸ And suddenly, having-looked-around, they-saw no-one any-longer, but Jesus alone with them.

⁹ And as-they-were-coming-down from the mountain, he-ordered them that they-should-relate to-no-one what they-saw, except when the Son of Man should-rise from [the] dead.

¹⁰ And they-held the word to themselves, discussing what the "to-rise from [the] dead" means.

¹¹ And they-were-asking him, saying, "Why do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?"

¹² And he said to-them, "Elijah indeed, having-come first, restores all-things. And how has-it-been-written concerning the Son of Man, that he-should-suffer many-[things] and be-treated-with-contempt?

¹³ But I-say to-you that Elijah also has-come, and they-did to-him whatever they-wanted, just-as it-has-been-written concerning him."

¹⁴ And having-come to the disciples, they-saw a-great crowd around them and scribes disputing with them.

¹⁵ And immediately all the crowd, having-seen him, were-amazed, and running-to [him], were-greeting him.

¹⁶ And he-asked them, "What are-you-disputing with them?"

¹⁷ And one out-of the crowd answered him, "Teacher, I-brought my son to you, having a-speechless spirit.

¹⁸ And wherever it-seizes him, it-throws him-down, and he-foams and grinds his teeth and becomes-rigid. And I-spoke to-your disciples in-order-that they-might-cast it-out, and they-were not strong-[enough]."

¹⁹ And he, answering them, says, "O unbelieving generation, how-long will-I-be with you? How-long will-I-bear-with you? Bring him to me."

²⁰ And they-brought him to him. And having-seen him, the spirit immediately convulsed him, and having-fallen on the ground, he-was-rolling, foaming.

²¹ And he-asked his father, "How-long a-time is-it since this has-happened to-him?" And he-said, "From childhood.

²² And often it-has-thrown him both into fire and into water in-order-that it-might-destroy him. But if you-are-able [to do] anything, have-compassion on us and help us."

²³ And Jesus said to-him, "The 'if you-are-able' — all-things [are] possible to-the-one believing."

²⁴ Immediately having-cried-out, the father of-the child was-saying, "I-believe! Help my unbelief!"

²⁵ And Jesus, having-seen that a-crowd was-running-together, rebuked the unclean spirit, saying to-it, "Speechless and deaf spirit, I command you, come-out of-him and no-longer enter into him."

²⁶ And having-cried-out and having-convulsed [him] greatly, it-came-out. And he-became as-if dead, so-that many were-saying that he-died.

²⁷ But Jesus, having-taken his hand, raised him, and he-stood-up.

²⁸ And having-entered into a-house, his disciples privately were-asking him, "Why were we not able to-cast it-out?"

²⁹ And he-said to-them, "This kind is-able to-come-out by nothing except by prayer."

³⁰ And having-gone-out from-there, they-were-passing-through Galilee, and he-did not want that anyone should-know,

³¹ for he-was-teaching his disciples and was-saying to-them, "The Son of Man is-being-delivered into [the] hands of-men, and they-will-kill him, and having-been-killed, after three days he-will-rise."

³² But they-did not understand the saying, and they-were-afraid to-ask him.

³³ And they-came to Capernaum. And having-been in the house, he-was-asking them, "What were-you-discussing on the way?"

³⁴ But they-were-silent, for on the way they-had-discussed with one-another who [was] greatest.

³⁵ And having-sat-down, he-called the twelve and says to-them, "If anyone wants to-be first, he-will-be last of-all and servant of-all."

³⁶ And having-taken a-child, he-set it in [the] midst of-them, and having-embraced it, he-said to-them,

³⁷ "Whoever receives one of such children in my name receives me; and whoever receives me, receives not me but the-one having-sent me."

³⁸ John said to-him, "Teacher, we-saw someone casting-out demons in your name, and we-were-hindering him, because he-was not following us."

³⁹ But Jesus said, "Do not hinder him, for there-is no-one who will-do a-mighty-work in my name and will-be-able quickly to-speak-evil-of me.

⁴⁰ For whoever is not against us is for us.

⁴¹ For whoever gives-you a-cup of-water to-drink in [the] name that you-are of-Christ — truly I-say to-you that he-will not lose his reward.

⁴² And whoever causes-to-stumble one of these little-ones believing [in me], it-is much-better for-him if a-millstone is-placed around his neck and he-has-been-thrown into the sea.

⁴³ And if your hand causes-you-to-stumble, cut it-off. It-is better for-you to-enter into life maimed than having two hands to-go-away into Gehenna, into the unquenchable fire.

⁴⁴ [Where their worm does not die and the fire is not quenched.]

⁴⁵ And if your foot causes-you-to-stumble, cut it-off. It-is better for-you to-enter into life lame than having two feet to-be-thrown into Gehenna.

⁴⁶ [Where their worm does not die and the fire is not quenched.]

⁴⁷ And if your eye causes-you-to-stumble, cast it-out. It-is better for-you to-enter one-eyed into the kingdom of God than having two eyes to-be-thrown into Gehenna,

⁴⁸ where their worm does not die and the fire is not quenched.

⁴⁹ For everyone will-be-salted with fire.

⁵⁰ Salt [is] good; but if the salt becomes unsalty, with-what will-you-season it? Have salt in yourselves and be-at-peace with one-another."

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **9:1 — Chapter division:** This verse is the conclusion of Jesus's discourse from 8:34-38. The chapter division is artificial; in the manuscript, the text flows continuously.

2. **9:3 — "a fuller" (γναφεύς):** One who bleaches or whitens cloth. Sinaiticus reads ΓΝΑΦΕΥΣ. Mark uses a vivid, earthly comparison for a heavenly event.

3. **9:23 — "The 'if you are able'":** Jesus throws the father's words back at him. The Greek is difficult — ΤΟ ΕΙ ΔΥΝΗ — literally "the 'if you are able.'" The article ΤΟ turns the father's conditional phrase into a quoted object. I preserve this awkward construction because it captures Jesus's rhetorical move.

4. **9:29 — "by prayer":** Sinaiticus reads ΠΡΟΣΕΥΧΗ alone ("prayer"). Many later manuscripts add ΚΑΙ ΝΗΣΤΕΙΑ ("and fasting"). Vaticanus also reads prayer alone. The addition of fasting reflects later ascetic practice.

5. **9:44, 46 — Bracketed verses:** These verses are absent from Sinaiticus and Vaticanus. They are later scribal additions that repeat v. 48 (from Isaiah 66:24). I bracket them as textually uncertain.

6. **9:49 — "salted with fire":** One of the most enigmatic sayings in Mark. The connection between salt and fire is obscure. The text in Sinaiticus reads ΠΑΣ ΓΑΡ ΠΥΡΙ ΑΛΙΣΘΗΣΕΤΑΙ.

— EMET, AI Translator
